🪦 team-size-gate shipped dead

Guarded on Task; hooks.json registers the matcher as Agent. It early-returned on every real spawn.
💀
The fan-out gate released in v8.72.0 (#2561) has had zero effect since release. CC renamed the Task tool to Agent, and hooks.json registers the PreToolUse dispatcher under the Agent matcher — so every live invocation carries tool_name: "Agent". The guard rejected anything that wasn't "Task". The ledger was never written. The advisory never fired.
0
spawns ever counted
100%
of invocations early-returned
✅ 12
tests passing… while dead
14
passing after fix (+ regression guard)

1 The bug

// hooks.json — CC renamed Task → Agent
PreToolUse: matcher = 'Agent'   → CC only invokes with tool_name: "Agent"

// team-size-gate.ts:87
if (input.tool_name && input.tool_name !== 'Task') return outputSilentSuccess();
                                            ↑
                          'Agent' !== 'Task'  →  ALWAYS early-return
                          the gate NEVER FIRES 💀

2 Why the tests didn't catch it

❌ before — the tests encoded the bug

// spawn() helper fed a value CC no longer sends
{ tool_name: 'Task', ... }
→ 12/12 green, hook stone dead

✅ after — the test reads the REAL matcher

// parse hooks.json, assert the gate COUNTS a spawn
// for every tool name it actually registers
it.each(matchersFrom('hooks.json'))(...)
→ a future rename FAILS here
   instead of silently killing the hook

3 Negative control — proof the test bites

guard = Set(['Agent','Task'])   →  14 passed ✅
guard = !== 'Task'  (reverted)  →  × fires for tool_name=Agent  ·  8 FAILED ❌
restored                     →  14 passed ✅

4 The pattern — this is a recurrence

This is the same class as the #959 dead-dispatch regression: a hook present in the registry but unreachable from hooks.json, silent for 4 months, which killed 3 telemetry streams.

⚠️ A parallel audit found 48 more silently-dead hooks in the entries map right now — including 10 dead telemetry writers. The systemic fix is a bidirectional registry check (entries-map ⊆ hooks.json ∪ frontmatter ∪ dispatcher-fanout) in CI. Tracked separately.

A hook that is registered but never dispatched is indistinguishable from a hook that works — unless something asserts it actually fires. That assertion is what this PR adds.
